title: Blini
subtitle: Russian yeasted buckwheat pancakes
excerpt: Small, thick pancakes made with buckwheat flour and a live yeast batter that gives them a faintly sour, deeply nutty flavour. Eaten warm with soured cream, smoked salmon, or caviar.
sourceNotes: Blini are one of the oldest Russian foods, associated with Maslenitsa (Shrovetide) and served with soured cream, caviar, and smoked fish. Documented in Elena Molokhovets, A Gift to Young Housewives (1861, modern reprint) and Lesley Chamberlain, The Food and Cooking of Eastern Europe (1989). The yeasted buckwheat version is the traditional form.

content.text: Real blini are yeasted. The batter ferments for an hour (or overnight), which develops a characteristic mild sourness that plain-flour pancakes lack. Buckwheat gives them an earthy, slightly bitter quality that is the whole point. It is what makes smoked salmon and soured cream taste so right alongside them.

The batter should be thicker than crêpe batter, closer to a thick double cream in consistency. Each blini should be about 7 to 8 cm across and a centimetre thick. They cook quickly: 90 seconds per side in a hot buttered pan.

items.name: Unsalted butter
items.prepNote: melted, plus more for the pan

content.text: Dissolve the yeast and sugar in the warm milk and leave for 5 minutes until foamy. In a large bowl, whisk together the buckwheat flour, plain flour, and salt. Add the yeast mixture, egg yolks, and melted butter. Whisk until smooth. Cover with cling film and leave in a warm place for 1 hour until the batter looks slightly bubbly.

Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks. Fold gently into the batter, this lightens the blini. Heat a small frying pan over medium heat and brush with butter. Drop a heaped tablespoon of batter into the pan for each blini (they should be about 7 cm across). Cook for 1.5 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface and the underside is golden. Flip and cook for 1 minute more. Keep warm. Serve with soured cream and smoked salmon.

items.fix: Whisk egg whites to soft peaks and fold in gently just before frying, this is essential for lightness
items.cause: Egg whites not folded in, or over-mixed after folding
items.symptom: Blini are too dense and heavy

items.fix: Milk should be warm but comfortable on the wrist, not hot; if the batter is flat, the yeast is dead and the batch needs to be remade
items.cause: Yeast was killed by milk that was too hot
items.symptom: Batter has not risen after an hour

content.text: Blini are the food of Maslenitsa, the Russian week before Lent. They are eaten at every meal that week. The round golden shape stands in for the sun and the return of spring. Beyond that week they are still a feast-day food. Smoked salmon, caviar, or herring on top, with soured cream alongside, is the usual run.
